input_note: single-channel featuremap: grayscale (channel mean) + InstanceNorm done on CPU before the board, not pixels. See export.py / calib.py.

outputs.note: Shapes derived from the 8x stride and heads in export.py; not independently re-measured from the shipped ONNX. Keypoint NMS, top-k selection and sparse descriptor sampling are CPU post-processing in BCDL, not in the graph.

quantisation: compiler default (no optimization directive) — predominantly int8. Not independently re-verified in this repo.
licence: XFeat (accelerated_features) is Apache-2.0. The pretrained xfeat.pt follows the same repo's terms — check before redistribution.

toolchain.note: Recorded so a future failure can be attributed. The host HBDK and the board HBRT must match; 3.7.0 host pairs with 4.0.5 board. Not confirmed against a fresh rebuild here.

cosine.note: Three outputs; run the A/C gate per output (feats/keypoints/heatmap). For a feature extractor the honest end-to-end check is the task metric below (keypoint repeatability + descriptor match count), because a plausible-looking descriptor cosine does not prove the matches survive — the same failure mode as OSNet.

task_metric.name: sparse feature match: mutual-nearest-neighbour matches (min cossim 0.82) between an image pair, and keypoint agreement vs the float decode
task_metric.protocol: Run the float ONNX decode (xfeat_ref.py: XFeat.get_kpts_heatmap -> NMS kernel 5 -> InterpolateSparse2d nearest*bilinear scoring -> top_k 4096, threshold 0.05, L2-normalised descriptors -> mutual-NN match) as the reference, then run the same CPU decode on the board .hbm outputs and compare kept keypoints and match count on the upstream assets/ref.png <-> assets/tgt.png pair.
task_metric.note: No board-measured match/repeatability numbers survived in the salvage material. The reference decode exists (reconstruct from xfeat_ref.py); the acceptance numbers were never recorded here. Do NOT treat descriptor cosine alone as the gate.

measured.note: No on-board latency / FPS / cosine figures survived in the salvage material for the shipped xfeat_nashm_640x480.hbm. Left null rather than invented; measure on an S100P with hrt_model_exec on a quiet board and fill in.

rejected_builds.name: InstanceNorm left in the graph
rejected_builds.why: XFeat's InstanceNorm is a per-image, data-dependent statistic that quantises badly. The shipped recipe lifts it into CPU preprocessing so the graph sees a standardised, single-channel featuremap. Reconstruction of intent from export.py; no side-by-side accuracy number was recorded.

graph_rewrites.why: data-dependent per-image stat, quantises badly; also standardises the input domain. Model input becomes an already grayscale + instance-normalised [1,1,480,640] featuremap.
graph_rewrites.verified: export.py checks the rewritten module vs the original end-to-end (max|diff| < 1e-4 on feats/keypoints/heatmap).

graph_rewrites.why: torch.onnx has no clean lowering for the custom window gather; pixel_unshuffle exports to a single SpaceToDepth and is the same permutation for C=1 (offset (dy,dx) -> channel dy*8+dx).
graph_rewrites.verified: export.py asserts torch.equal(_unfold2d(probe,8), pixel_unshuffle(probe,8)) before export.

board_verification.note: No three-layer close-out survived in the salvage material for this model. The recipe reproduces the export (with its two numerically-checked rewrites) and the calibration method; on-board A/B/C and the task metric still need to be run and recorded.
